evidence: Packet architecture evidence flags `src/shared/lib/errorHandling/runtimeError.ts` (259 importers) and `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` (154 importers) as top hubs, confirming high blast-radius modules.

Concrete boundary drift is present: feature/domain/shared/tool modules directly import `@/integrations/supabase/client` (e.g., `src/features/resources/hooks/useResourceBrowserData.ts`, `src/features/billing/hooks/useCredits.ts`, `src/domains/generation/repository/derivedItemsRepository.ts`, many others), bypassing integration-owned repositories.

Deferred-import pressure is confirmed by cycle-avoidance patterns (e.g., `src/shared/lib/logger.ts` dynamically imports Supabase client with explicit circular-dependency comment; `src/integrations/supabase/auth/AuthStateManager.ts` dynamically imports reconnect scheduler during auth handling).

Import-time side-effect concern is only partially supported: inspected seed/runtime files do not perform module-scope network I/O; runtime bootstrap is mostly explicit (`initializeSupabase*`), so the dominant defect is boundary erosion and cycle pressure rather than startup side effects.

`holistic_context.structure.directory_profiles["src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/"]` has 49 files / 9605 LOC with wide import spread across shared hooks/lib/media, domain types, and Supabase, indicating a subsystem-level catch-all package.

`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/index.ts` re-exports many hooks across unrelated concerns (inpainting, edit persistence, task details, video editing, navigation, layout), creating a broad public surface with weak ownership boundaries.

`buildImageEditStateValue.ts` composes a very large state surface from multiple mode hooks while `editSettingsTypes.ts` defines durable settings models in the same hooks package, mixing orchestration, domain settings, and UI hook concerns.

Directory profile shows this package is consumed by both `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/*` and `src/tools/edit-images/hooks/`, confirming the folder acts as cross-feature integration storage rather than a clear capability boundary.

`src/shared/services/externalApiKeys/` mixes three public error contracts for one flow: verifier returns `{valid,error}`, repository throws (`fetchExternalApiKey`/`saveExternalApiKey`), and hook returns `{success,error}` in `saveToken`, forcing callers to translate between incompatible conventions.

`src/shared/components/TaskDetails/TaskDetailsModal.tsx` and `src/shared/components/TaskDetails/TaskDetailsPanel.tsx` implement near-identical UI/interaction blocks (copy id/params, detailed-json toggle, apply-settings controls) instead of one shared convention for task-details presentation.

Blind packet fields requested for this batch (`holistic_context.errors.exception_hotspots`, `holistic_context.conventions.naming_drift`, `behavioral_outliers`) are null, so convention confirmation required direct code sampling; no packet-backed counterexample showed a stronger unifying naming standard in these modules.

`runOptimisticCacheUpdate` catches and suppresses errors (`showToast: false`) and does not propagate failure, yet callers continue to persist selection (`useResourceSelectHandler`, `useStyleReferenceUploadHandler`) as if optimistic state update succeeded.

`persistReferenceSelection` reads current cache and writes it to DB; if optimistic mutation failed silently, persistence commits stale references while caller path still marks interaction as successful.

`useGenerationDetails` converts JSON parse failures into empty `inputImages` and suppresses toast; `useGenerationTaskDetails` similarly logs mapping errors with `showToast: false` and returns no-task-like state, collapsing operational failure and legitimate "no details" into the same UX outcome.

`MediaLightbox` accepts a very wide flat API, then reconstructs grouped seam objects (`navigation`, `shotWorkflow`, `features`, `actions`, `videoProps`) before delegating to `ImageLightbox`/`VideoLightbox`; this repeated boundary translation is glue-code entropy rather than a stable contract.

`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/types.ts` defines grouped contracts, but `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/MediaLightbox.tsx` reintroduces flat interfaces (`*FlatProps`) and remaps them, creating a two-shape boundary that is easy to drift.
Task-details orchestration is split across modal/panel variants with duplicated control logic instead of a shared presenter/controller seam, increasing integration churn when behavior changes.

Mechanical pass-through wrapper signal is confirmed: `generationMutationsRepository.ts`, `projectSetupRepository.ts`, and `homeAuthRepository.ts` mostly forward directly to `getSupabaseClient().from/rpc` with minimal behavioral ownership.

Abstraction contract is split between throwing and non-throwing Supabase access paths: repositories call `getSupabaseClient()` (throws) while hooks like `useTasks.ts` and `useAuthReferralFinalize.ts` use `getSupabaseClientResult()` and add local guard logic.

Auth referral behavior is duplicated in multiple abstractions (`useHomeAuth.ts` inline flow vs `useAuthReferralFinalize.ts` + `homeAuthRepository.ts`), creating parallel implementations of the same business operation.
No delegation-heavy classes, facade modules, or TypedDict violations were found in this scope (consistent with packet abstraction metadata).

Preloading is directly coupled to realtime status: `PreloadingService.preloadImages` hard-returns when `isConnected` is false, and connection state is sourced from `DataFreshnessManager.realtimeStatus === 'connected'`.
On disconnect, `PreloadingService.onConnectionStatusChange` clears the queue (`queue.clear()`), and there is no replay mechanism for dropped preload work after reconnect.
This creates a hard dependency where realtime instability can disable unrelated media prefetch behavior, even when ordinary HTTP fetches still work.
`useShotEditorController.ts` remains a high-local-complexity orchestrator (878 LOC hotspot in packet) with large object construction and broad hook wiring in one function body.
The local helper `buildShotEditorGenerationControllerInput` is a thin repacking layer that does not reduce cognitive load, while callers still assemble deeply nested argument bags.
This local ceremony raises change risk because editing one behavior requires touching large contiguous wiring regions with many coupled fields.

Critical hook `src/shared/hooks/tasks/useTaskType.ts` has no direct test file, while it is consumed by UI-critical callers (`src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem.tsx`, `src/shared/components/TasksPane/TaskItem.tsx`).

Current `useTaskInvalidation` test suite (`src/shared/hooks/invalidation/__tests__/useTaskInvalidation.test.ts`) validates query-key shapes and raw `queryClient.invalidateQueries` calls but does not exercise any production invalidation function, because `src/shared/hooks/invalidation/useTaskInvalidation.ts` is only a type stub.

Seed mechanical concern 'critical untested paths' is partially confirmed: some seed files are covered (e.g. `adjacentPagePreloadCoordinator.test.ts`, `timelineWriteQueue.test.ts`, `referenceDomainService.test.ts`), but task-type fetch/invalidation seams are not tested end-to-end.

Two public query-key APIs represent generation->task mapping in parallel: `taskQueryKeys.generationTaskId(...)` and `taskQueryKeys.generationMapping(...)` (`src/shared/lib/queryKeys/tasks.ts`), and both are actively used by different modules (`useTaskPrefetch.ts` vs `generationTaskCache.ts`).

Task detail cache keys are also split: readers/prefetchers commonly use `taskQueryKeys.single(taskId, projectId)` (`useTasks.ts`, `useTaskPrefetch.ts`), but `generationTaskCache.ts` reads/writes `taskQueryKeys.single(taskId)` with no project scope, creating `__no-project__` cache islands.

Task-type API masks failure as absence: `useTaskType` returns `null` for both DB errors and missing rows, and `fetchAllTaskTypesConfig` returns `{}` on error, collapsing distinct states into the same shape.

src/tools/travel-between-images/components/Timeline/hooks/timeline-core/useTimelinePositions.ts carries unusually dense narrative comments around nearly every branch plus placeholder sections like "BACKWARDS COMPATIBILITY" with no implementation, which adds maintenance noise without executable value.

src/tools/travel-between-images/hooks/video/useStableSkeletonVisibility.ts and src/tools/travel-between-images/hooks/video/useTemporaryVisibility.ts include long tutorial-style comments for straightforward hooks, repeating behavior already explicit in code.

Dual API surface persists for structure-video data: src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/types.ts defines both legacy primaryStructureVideo* fields and canonical structureVideos array in the same state contract.

src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/hooks/video/useStructureVideo.ts returns both array mutators and legacy primary accessors, preserving two representations instead of one canonical internal model.

src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/services/applySettings/taskDataService.ts still fans in multiple legacy keys (structure_video_path, structure_video_type, structure_type) alongside canonical structure_videos parsing, indicating migration remains open-ended rather than boundary-contained.
Mechanical concern from prior review is confirmed: ShotImagesEditorImageState remains bloated and largely due to this legacy+canonical overlap.

Mechanical batch concern signals are empty (`investigation_batches[name=Package Organization].concern_signals = []`), and `holistic_context.scan_evidence.boundary_violations` is null, so there is no direct detector-confirmed boundary break to report.

`holistic_context.structure.directory_profiles["src/shared/hooks/"]` shows 48 files and mixed responsibilities in one package (UI interaction hooks like `useFileDragTracking.ts` alongside data-access/domain hooks like `useLineageChain.ts`, `usePendingGenerationTasks.ts`, `useShareGeneration.ts`).

`holistic_context.structure.directory_profiles["src/shared/lib/"]` shows 54 files with mixed generic utilities (`clipboard.ts`, `fileConversion.ts`, `uuid.ts`) and generation/task repositories/cache (`generationTaskCache.ts`, `generationTaskRepository.ts`, `taskRowMapper.ts`) in one directory.

`src/shared/hooks/useLineageChain.ts` performs direct Supabase reads and lineage traversal while living in the same top-level package as generic UI hooks, indicating placement by convenience rather than domain boundary.

Coupling matrix shows strong dependence `src/shared/hooks/ -> src/shared/lib/` (30 edges) and `src/shared/hooks/ -> src/integrations/supabase/` (21 edges), consistent with a broad shared layer collecting domain code.

The blind packet has no populated `holistic_context.scan_evidence.mutable_globals` / `holistic_context.errors.mutable_globals` entries, so mechanical mutable-global evidence is absent and had to be validated manually in code.

`useJoinClips` writes cross-feature workflow state directly to `localStorage` (`pendingJoinClips...` keys), while `useClipManager` reads and consumes that queue later; this is shared mutable browser-global state with implicit lifecycle ordering (`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useJoinClips.ts`, `src/tools/join-clips/lib/clipInitService.ts`, `src/tools/join-clips/hooks/useClipManager.ts`).

`useClipManager` consumes pending join clips only when `[settingsLoaded, selectedProjectId, initialHydrationComplete]` change; there is no storage/event subscription for later writes, so queue consumption depends on init timing/remount rather than an explicit registry contract (`src/tools/join-clips/hooks/useClipManager.ts:99-135`).

Cross-component state signaling also uses global `window` events (`dispatchAppEvent`/`useAppEventListener`) with no replay/buffering contract, making delivery order depend on listener mount timing (`src/shared/lib/typedEvents.ts`, `src/features/gallery/hooks/useGalleryPageState.ts`, `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/VideoGallery/SortableShotItem.tsx`).
Confirmed mechanical concern: `src/integrations/supabase/types.ts` is still a 2576-line monolith and is imported by 24 production files, so DB typing concerns are centralized in one oversized module.

Confirmed mechanical concern: `src/pages/Home/hooks/useHomeAuth.ts` combines URL token parsing, session bootstrap, PWA redirect policy, auth subscription, referral side effects, and navigation in a single effect with nested async closures.

Refuted mechanical concern: `src/domains/generation/mappers/generationParamsMapper.ts` has branching, but the branches are cohesive validation/mapping rules and extracted helpers (`sanitizePayloadField`, `validateOrchestratorDetailsPayload`, `toGenerationParams`, `toPersistedGenerationParams`) rather than mixed responsibilities.

Partially confirmed mechanical concern: deep nested closures in `src/domains/generation/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts` remain (factory + branch + mutation callbacks), but this file is substantially decomposed into module-level helpers and mostly reflects query/mutation orchestration.

Refuted mechanical concern: `src/integrations/supabase/bootstrap/fetchWithTimeout.ts` is compact and purpose-specific; nested closures are limited to abort composition and cleanup behavior and do not indicate broader design drift.

Cross-file duplication pattern: auth/session workflows are split across `src/shared/contexts/AuthContext.tsx`, `src/pages/Home/hooks/useHomeAuth.ts`, and `src/shared/components/GlobalHeader/useGlobalHeaderAuth.ts`, each independently handling session fetch/subscription and side effects.
Most public contracts in the reviewed scope use intent-revealing names (for example `useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm`, `resolveSettingsScopeTable`, `buildBatchTaskParams`).
Minor drift remains where names suggest narrower behavior than implementation, e.g. `getUser` in `useResourceBrowserData` actually pulls session state (`auth.getSession`) rather than a user object.
naming_quality.issues_preventing_higher_score: A few helper names still blur behavior boundaries (session vs user retrieval), which adds avoidable cognitive load in already state-heavy hooks.

evidence: Confirmed mechanical hotspot relevance: `src/shared/components/PhaseConfigSelectorModal/PhaseConfigVertical.tsx` is large and stateful; logic paths are difficult to audit quickly.
`useResourceBrowserData` performs `setCurrentPage(1)` during render when filter state changes (lines 66-70), introducing render-phase state mutation and non-obvious control flow.
`useImageTransform` casts untrusted persisted data (`activeVariantParams?.transform as ImageTransform`) and hydrates state from it without runtime shape/type guards.

`useCanvasTransform` assumes all transform fields are valid numbers/booleans and uses them directly in CSS/canvas math (`translate`, `scale`, `rotate`), so malformed persisted payloads can propagate invalid numeric values.
`useListPublicResources` and `useListResources` are list-all style hooks but silently truncate at 20,000 rows via safety breaks, returning partial data with no error/flag.
Consumers (`useResourceBrowserData`, `ResourceBrowserGrid`) treat returned lists as complete datasets for filtering/pagination, so hidden truncation violates caller expectations.
`useUpdateResource` first queries resource existence without owner filter (`.eq('id', id)`), then returns owner-distinguishing errors (`This resource belongs to another user`).
The same function logs `resourceOwner` and `resourceExists` in error metadata, reinforcing object-enumeration behavior rather than consistently returning a generic unauthorized/not-found response.

issues.summary: Supabase runtime client is consumed directly across layers instead of through domain/integration boundaries.
issues.suggestion: Introduce integration-owned query/mutation facades (per domain slice), then ban direct imports of `@/integrations/supabase/client` outside `src/integrations/**` via lint rule + staged migration. Keep only bootstrap/auth-guard level accessors as explicit exceptions.
evidence: `useResourceBrowserData` calls `getSupabaseClient().auth.getSession()` directly inside feature UI data orchestration.
Many non-integration modules import `@/integrations/supabase/client` directly (features/domains/shared/tools), turning integration runtime into a global dependency surface.
This pattern bypasses integration-owned repository seams and makes any client/runtime contract change codebase-wide.

issues.summary: A single shared error runtime is coupled into integration internals, blending infra and app-layer concerns.
issues.suggestion: Split error handling into strict layers: `normalize/report` (infra-safe, no presenter dependency) and `present` (UI/app). Integration code should depend only on infra-safe layer; app/hook boundaries can opt into presentation.
evidence: Integration modules (`createSupabaseClient`, `AuthStateManager`) call `normalizeAndPresentError` from shared runtime error hub.
`runtimeError` routes through `presentRuntimeError`, whose presenter is installed at app bootstrap, so infra modules are coupled to app-level presentation/error policy plumbing.
With 259 importers, this hub centralizes unrelated layer concerns and amplifies architectural blast radius.

issues.summary: ImageGenerationForm context and hook module form an explicit 2-file cycle.
issues.suggestion: Break the cycle by moving context token/types into a neutral `contextTypes.ts` (or `contextToken.ts`) and have both files import from that module; keep re-export barrel separate from the context definition file.
evidence: `ImageGenerationFormContext.tsx` re-exports hooks from `hooks/useImageGenerationFormContexts`.
`useImageGenerationFormContexts.ts` imports `ImageGenerationFormContext` back from `../ImageGenerationFormContext`.
Packet dependency evidence reports one existing cycle matching this pair.

issues.summary: Project state ownership stays ambiguous because legacy combined hook coexists with split-context API.
issues.suggestion: Define a migration end-state and deprecate `useProject()` with phased codemods: migrate consumers to narrow hooks per responsibility, then remove combined facade and enforce API usage by lint rule.
evidence: `ProjectContext` defines separated Selection/Crud/Identity contexts but still exports `useProject()` that recombines all of them for compatibility.
Consumers are mixed: some use narrow hooks (`useProjectSelectionContext`), while many pages/tools still consume broad `useProject()`.
This keeps dependency boundaries unclear and encourages broad coupling to project subsystem internals.

issues.summary: Dynamic imports are being used as cycle-pressure workarounds in core runtime paths.
issues.suggestion: Extract small, dependency-inverted interfaces for logging transport and reconnect scheduling, inject them at bootstrap, and remove runtime dynamic imports from steady-state paths.
evidence: `logger.flushLogs` performs `await import('@/integrations/supabase/client')` with an inline note to avoid circular dependencies.
`AuthStateManager` dynamically imports reconnect scheduler during auth flow, introducing runtime indirection in core integration path.
These deferred imports indicate unresolved dependency direction issues around shared runtime singletons.

issues.summary: Optimistic reference-update failures are swallowed, then stale data is persisted as success
issues.suggestion: Return an explicit `OperationResult` from `runOptimisticCacheUpdate` and gate persistence/interaction success on `ok`. If optimistic update fails, abort persistence and surface a user-visible recovery path (retry or hard refresh of reference state).
evidence: `runOptimisticCacheUpdate` wraps `operation()` in try/catch and never rethrows.
Both upload/select handlers call `runOptimisticCacheUpdate(...)` and immediately proceed to `persistReferenceSelection(...)` and then `markAsInteracted()`.
`persistReferenceSelection` snapshots cache state via `queryClient.getQueryData(...)`; if optimistic mutation failed, old state is persisted without surfacing a hard failure.

issues.summary: Task-details retrieval/parsing errors are flattened into normal empty-state behavior
issues.suggestion: Introduce a distinct `failure_state` in task-details contracts (e.g., `status: 'ok' | 'missing' | 'error'`) and render an error-specific UI path when mapping/parse fails. Preserve original error context for telemetry and optional user feedback.
evidence: `useGenerationDetails` catches parse exceptions and returns `[]` images with `showToast: false`.
`useGenerationTaskDetails` catches on-demand mapping errors and reports silently (`showToast: false`) while returning task-null states used by UI.
Modal/panel render "No task details available" for `!task`, which is the same output used for genuine absence of task details.

issues.summary: MediaLightbox uses dual boundary shapes (flat and grouped), creating avoidable seam translation
issues.suggestion: Standardize on grouped seam contracts at the public `MediaLightbox` boundary and remove flat re-mapping interfaces. Keep a single canonical prop shape shared across container and child lightboxes.
evidence: `types.ts` already defines grouped seam interfaces (`LightboxNavigationProps`, `LightboxShotWorkflowProps`, etc.).
`MediaLightbox.tsx` defines additional flat prop interfaces and reconstructs grouped objects each render before delegation.
This produces two parallel API conventions for the same module boundary, increasing maintenance cost when adding/removing fields.

issues.summary: HuggingFace key flow mixes throw/result/success-object conventions without one canonical boundary
issues.suggestion: Adopt one error contract for this subsystem (prefer `OperationResult` or throw-based with a single adapter at hook boundary). Document and enforce it across verifier, repository, and hook layers.
evidence: `verifyHuggingFaceToken` never throws and returns `{valid,error}`.
Repository functions (`fetchExternalApiKey`, `saveExternalApiKey`, `deleteExternalApiKey`) throw errors directly.
Hook-level `saveToken` converts again into `{success,error}`, creating convention drift for adjacent callers and tests.

issues.summary: TaskDetails modal and panel duplicate orchestration logic instead of sharing a seam
issues.suggestion: Extract a shared task-details presenter/controller (state + handlers + common sections) and let modal/panel provide only layout shell concerns.
evidence: Both components duplicate copy-id/copy-params interactions, detailed-params expand/collapse, loading/empty rendering, and apply-settings controls.
Behavior differences are currently incidental (layout wrapper), not domain-specific, so duplicated orchestration adds synchronization risk when task-details behavior evolves.

issues.summary: Supabase access abstraction is split between throw-first repositories and result-checked hooks.
issues.suggestion: Unify on one data-access contract: either make repositories return normalized `OperationResult` values (non-throwing) or enforce throw-only access and centralize normalization in one adapter layer. Then migrate hooks to that single contract and remove duplicated runtime-init guards.
evidence: `getSupabaseClient()` throws on uninitialized runtime in `client.ts`, but many repositories call it directly and return raw query builders.
`useTasks.ts` and other hooks use `getSupabaseClientResult()` and add local fallback/error handling, duplicating guard behavior outside repository boundaries.
`useGenerationMutations.ts` must add extra validation/error choreography around thin repository calls, indicating low-leverage indirection.

issues.summary: Referral finalization logic is duplicated across hooks with divergent abstractions.
issues.suggestion: Collapse referral finalization into one shared service/hook contract (single storage key policy + single RPC call path) and have both Home auth entry points call that contract instead of maintaining separate implementations.
evidence: `useHomeAuth.ts` performs inline referral storage read/cleanup and calls `rpcCreateReferralFromSession` through repository flow.
`useAuthReferralFinalize.ts` separately implements referral read/call/cleanup logic but bypasses `homeAuthRepository.ts` and calls `supabase.rpc` directly.
Two parallel implementations of the same business step create drift risk for behavior, telemetry context, and error policy.

issues.summary: Image preloading is disabled by realtime connectivity state and drops queued work.
issues.suggestion: Decouple preloading eligibility from realtime channel health. Keep preloading active with throttled concurrency during realtime outages, and add a replay buffer so cleared pending preload intents can be retried after reconnect.
evidence: `PreloadingService.subscribeToConnectionStatus` derives connectivity from `DataFreshnessManager` realtime status, not from image-fetch capability.
`preloadImages()` returns immediately when `isConnected` is false, so transient realtime issues suppress preloading entirely.
`onConnectionStatusChange(false)` clears pending queue items, and no replay mechanism restores cleared work when connection recovers.

issues.summary: Timeline position ownership is fragmented across multiple overlapping core hooks.
issues.suggestion: Define one authoritative timeline write/read orchestrator (state + persistence + invalidation). Keep UI hooks as adapters over that core API, and move shared persistence semantics (optimistic policy, rollback, refetch) into the single orchestrator.
evidence: `useTimelineCore.ts` declares itself as timeline position source-of-truth, while `useTimelinePositions.ts` also declares single source-of-truth for positions.
`useTimelineFrameUpdates.ts` introduces a third persistence path for timeline updates that shares queue/RPC dependencies but not state ownership.
This causes seam friction: similar invalidation/persist choreography is repeated in separate modules and coordinated indirectly from container-level composition.

issues.summary: Shot editor controller builds and forwards oversized nested argument bags in one module.
issues.suggestion: Split `useShotEditorController` into focused composition hooks by concern (generation, media management, UI state bridge) and replace broad nested bags with narrower typed contracts created near each consumer.
evidence: `useShotEditorController.ts` wires a very large set of settings/actions and constructs a deep `generationControllerInput` object before passing it onward.
`buildShotEditorGenerationControllerInput` only repackages fields without reducing branch/ownership complexity.
Local changes require editing dense wiring regions with many coupled fields, increasing error-prone edits and review burden.

issues.summary: Generation->task mapping has two incompatible public cache key contracts
issues.suggestion: Converge on one canonical key (`generationMapping` or `generationTaskId`) and remove the other. Update all readers/writers and tests to the canonical key, then add a migration test that verifies prefetch and merge paths share the same cache entry.
evidence: `taskQueryKeys` exposes both `generationTaskId(generationId)` and `generationMapping(generationId)`.
`useTaskPrefetch` reads/writes `generationTaskId` while `generationTaskCache` reads/writes `generationMapping` for the same conceptual data (taskId + status).
This duplicates cache entries and allows stale/missing reads depending on which API path populated first.

issues.summary: Task cache uses mixed scoped/unscoped keys for the same entity
issues.suggestion: Make project scope mandatory for all task-detail cache access and carry `projectId` through `generationTaskCache` APIs. Replace unscoped `single(taskId)` calls with `single(taskId, projectId)` and add contract tests across `generationTaskCache` + `useTasks` + `useTaskPrefetch`.
evidence: `generationTaskCache` prefetches and reads task data with `taskQueryKeys.single(taskId)` (project omitted => `__no-project__`).
`useGetTask` and task prefetch hooks use `taskQueryKeys.single(taskId, effectiveProjectId)` and query by both `id` and `project_id`.
The mismatch means cross-module consumers can miss prefetched data or read divergent entries for the same task id.

issues.summary: Task-type fetch boundary lacks direct tests despite high fan-out
issues.suggestion: Add `useTaskType` tests covering success, DB error, missing row, and cache-init behavior for `useAllTaskTypesConfig`; include one integration-style test from a consuming component to validate UI behavior under each case.
evidence: No `useTaskType` test file exists under `src/shared/hooks/tasks`.
The hook is used in multiple user-facing components to determine task display behavior.
The hook intentionally collapses error/not-found to `null`, but there is no explicit regression coverage for this contract.

issues.summary: Task invalidation tests assert constants while production module is a stub
issues.suggestion: Either remove the dead module/tests entirely or implement and export real task invalidation functions, then rewrite tests to execute that implementation (including list/detail/counts/all scopes and generation-cascade behavior).
evidence: `useTaskInvalidation.ts` contains only type/interface declarations (no exported behavior).
The test suite still passes by asserting key literals and direct `queryClient.invalidateQueries` calls, not by invoking a production invalidation API.
This creates false confidence around task invalidation behavior.

issues.summary: Serialized timeline queue times out callers but cannot stop underlying write work
issues.suggestion: Change `runSerializedTimelineWrite` to accept an abortable task signature `(signal) => Promise<T>` and propagate cancellation on timeout. Require all write callers to use abort-aware DB paths, and add a test proving timed-out write side effects cannot complete after queue release.
evidence: `runSerializedTaskWithTimeout` uses timer-based reject but does not pass an abort signal into `task`.
When timeout fires, queue bookkeeping is released in `finally`, allowing next writes to start even if previous async DB operation continues.
Callers depend on queue ordering for timeline correctness; overlapping late completions can reintroduce stale invalidation/refetch churn.

issues.summary: Structure-video migration is stalled by maintaining two concurrent state contracts
issues.suggestion: Make `structure_videos` the only internal state contract across editor/timeline code. Move legacy key parsing into one adapter at ingestion boundaries, then remove `primaryStructureVideo*` fields from shared props and context interfaces.
evidence: ShotImagesEditorImageState includes legacy single-video fields (primaryStructureVideoPath/type/motion) and new structureVideos array simultaneously.
useStructureVideo exposes both canonical array operations and legacy primary-field accessors in its return type.
taskDataService still resolves structure settings by checking new array first, then multiple legacy top-level aliases.

issues.summary: Legacy-named default config is still used as canonical runtime source
issues.suggestion: Introduce a canonical `DEFAULT_STRUCTURE_VIDEO` object (`treatment`, `motion_strength`, `structure_type`, `uni3c_end_percent`) and derive legacy aliases only inside migration adapters. Replace direct `structure_video_*` default usage in editor hooks.
evidence: defaults.ts defines `DEFAULT_VIDEO_STRUCTURE_PARAMS` with legacy key names (`structure_video_*`) and comment indicates partial support assumptions.
useStructureVideo uses those legacy-key defaults directly when producing canonical structureVideos state and derived accessors.
payloadBuilder rejects implicit legacy fields in canonical mode, so default configuration semantics and canonical payload semantics are still split.

issues.summary: useTimelinePositions is a monolithic state machine with mixed responsibilities
issues.suggestion: Split into focused units: `useTimelinePositionState` (optimistic state + rollback), `useTimelinePositionPersistence` (DB writes + invalidation), and `useTimelinePositionSync` (server merge/guards). Keep `useTimelinePositions` as a thin composition wrapper.
evidence: The hook is 701 LOC and handles sync-from-server, optimistic state mutation, rollback, persistence, locking, queue-guard logic, and trailing endpoint persistence in one file.
It contains lifecycle/compatibility scaffolding and dead/noise artifacts (unused `_queryClient`, empty compatibility section) that indicate high local indirection cost.
Orchestrator-level interaction logic still depends on this mixed hook, coupling UI behavior to persistence internals.

issues.summary: Defensive explanatory scaffolding is drowning signal in timeline/video hooks
issues.suggestion: Prune comments to invariants and non-obvious race guarantees only; remove empty section scaffolding; keep API docs short and move deeper rationale to a single module-level note if needed.
evidence: useTimelinePositions includes extensive prose blocks repeating implementation details at many points, plus placeholder section headers without code.
Small hooks with simple behavior include long tutorial-style doc blocks that duplicate obvious control flow.
The pattern increases review overhead and makes true control-path changes harder to spot.

issues.summary: `src/shared/hooks` mixes generic UI hooks with generation/task data-access hooks, blurring package boundaries.
issues.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: Stage 1 create target folders `src/domains/generation/hooks/` and `src/domains/task/hooks/`; Stage 2 move data-centric hooks first (`useLineageChain`, `usePendingGenerationTasks`, `useShareGeneration`) into domain folders while keeping temporary re-export shims in `src/shared/hooks/`; Stage 3 move remaining domain hooks (variants/shot-generation/task-*); Stage 4 keep `src/shared/hooks/` for cross-domain UI/interaction-only hooks. After each stage run import updates via `rg -l "@/shared/hooks/use(LineageChain|PendingGenerationTasks|ShareGeneration)" src` and replace paths, then validate with `npm run typecheck` and `npm test -- useLineageChain`.
evidence: `useFileDragTracking.ts` is a generic drag-state utility, while `useLineageChain.ts` contains Supabase lineage traversal and project scope validation in the same directory.
`useShareGeneration.ts` and `usePendingGenerationTasks.ts` execute DB/auth queries from the same package that also holds local UI interaction hooks.
Directory profile: `src/shared/hooks/` has 48 files and imports from both `src/shared/lib/` (30 edges) and `src/integrations/supabase/` (21 edges), signaling mixed layer ownership.

issues.summary: `src/shared/lib` combines generic helpers and domain repositories/caches, reducing directory cohesion.
issues.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: Stage 1 create `src/domains/generation/repository/`, `src/domains/task/repository/`, and `src/shared/utils/browser/`; Stage 2 move `generationTaskCache.ts`, `generationTaskRepository.ts`, `taskRowMapper.ts` into domain repository folders; Stage 3 move generic helpers (`clipboard.ts`, `fileConversion.ts`) into `shared/utils/browser`; Stage 4 leave compatibility exports in `src/shared/lib/index` for one release, then remove. Update imports with `rg -l "@/shared/lib/(generationTaskCache|generationTaskRepository|taskRowMapper|clipboard|fileConversion)" src` and validate using `npm run typecheck` plus focused tests `npm test -- generationTaskCache`.
evidence: Generic browser/file helpers (`clipboard.ts`, `fileConversion.ts`) are co-located with generation/task persistence and query-cache orchestration (`generationTaskCache.ts`, `generationTaskRepository.ts`).
`generationTaskCache.ts` depends on Supabase client and task query keys, i.e., domain/integration behavior rather than shared primitive utility.
Directory profile: `src/shared/lib/` has 54 files and is heavily imported by diverse directories, which increases blast radius of changes in a nominally 'shared' package.

issues.summary: MediaLightbox hooks package has become a multi-purpose subsystem hub with unclear ownership boundaries.
issues.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: Stage 1 split `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/` into subpackages `hooks/orchestration/`, `hooks/modes/`, `hooks/persistence/`, and `model/` (for settings types); Stage 2 move files in dependency-safe order: types/models first (`editSettingsTypes.ts`), then pure state builders (`buildImageEditStateValue.ts`), then mode hooks, then orchestration hooks (`useSharedLightboxState.ts`, controller-level hooks); Stage 3 replace broad barrel with scoped entrypoints (`hooks/modes/index.ts`, `hooks/orchestration/index.ts`) and keep a temporary deprecated root barrel. Update imports via `rg -l "@/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks" src` and validate with `npm run typecheck` and `npm test -- MediaLightbox/hooks`.
evidence: Directory profile reports 49 files / 9605 LOC for one hooks package, with inbound/outbound coupling across shared hooks/lib/media, domain types, and integrations.
`index.ts` exposes a broad mixed-scope API (edit orchestration, persistence, task detail hooks, video mode hooks), making ownership and dependency intent hard to infer.
`buildImageEditStateValue.ts` and `editSettingsTypes.ts` sit together despite representing different layers (state assembly orchestration vs durable settings model).
This package is consumed from both MediaLightbox components and external tool hooks (`src/tools/edit-images/hooks/`), confirming it acts as a cross-feature bucket.

issues.summary: Join-clips handoff relies on ambient localStorage queue with init-timing dependent consumption
issues.suggestion: Replace the ambient queue with a dedicated `JoinClipsIntentStore` (React-query cache slice or context-backed registry) that provides `enqueue`, `subscribe`, and `ack` semantics. Keep localStorage only as persistence fallback behind that store, and trigger consumption through explicit store events rather than init-only effects.
evidence: `useJoinClips` writes pending clip entries directly to `localStorage` (`localStorage.getItem`/`setItem`) using queue keys from `getPendingJoinClipsStorageKey`.

`useClipManager` consumes pending clips in an effect gated by `settingsLoaded`, `selectedProjectId`, and `initialHydrationComplete`; once those are stable, later queue writes are not automatically observed.
`consumePendingJoinClips` destructively removes queue keys after read, so behavior depends on which consumer instance initializes first.

issues.summary: Upload feedback state is decoupled from mutation outcome, causing misleading UI transitions
issues.suggestion: Move shot-upload feedback to a single transaction model: emit `started/succeeded/failed` events (or, better, share mutation status via central state) keyed by operation id. Update `SortableShotItem` from explicit operation state instead of inferred count/timeouts.
evidence: `dispatchAppEvent('shot-pending-upload', ...)` is fired before `addImageToShotMutation`/`positionExistingGenerationMutation` resolves (`useGalleryPageState.ts:148-166`).

`SortableShotItem` immediately enters loading state on this event and infers completion from image-count deltas/timeouts (`SortableShotItem.tsx:195-215`, `:116-143`, `:162-179`), not from actual mutation result.
On mutation failure path in `useGalleryPageState`, no compensating event or rollback signal is emitted, so the drop indicator can display loading/success despite failure.

issues.summary: Image lightbox orchestration aggregates too many concerns into one unstable control surface
issues.suggestion: Introduce bounded feature controllers with narrow interfaces (e.g., `variantController`, `editController`, `shotController`, `panelController`) and compose them via a typed coordinator that only exposes stable cross-cutting contracts. This should remove wide prop bags and reduce re-render churn from unrelated concern changes.
evidence: `ImageLightbox` builds and threads large composite models (`env`, `sharedModel`, `editModel`, `renderModel`) with broad dependency arrays, mixing concerns: variant lifecycle, shot workflows, edit orchestration, task detail panels, layout, and navigation (`ImageLightbox.tsx:340-862`).

`useSharedLightboxState` exports a very wide return contract containing variants/navigation/star/references/lineage/shot creation/source generation/layout/button groups, increasing coupling pressure across callers.
`useInlineEditState` mirrors similar broad orchestration for edit modes/persistence/variants/lora/upscale, indicating repeated controller-aggregation pattern rather than bounded feature seams.

issues.summary: Tool settings auth cache is not cleared on sign-out, allowing stale user identity during initialization-time settings fetch.
issues.suggestion: Add an explicit `clearCachedUserId()` API in `toolSettingsService` and call it on signed-out/null-session transitions in `AuthContext`; additionally key or invalidate inflight settings fetches on auth change to prevent stale completion writes.
evidence: `toolSettingsService.ts` keeps module-level `cachedUser`/`cachedUserAt` and `getCachedUserId()` returns cache first when within `USER_CACHE_MS`.
`AuthContext.tsx` calls `setCachedUserId(currentUserId)` only when `currentUserId` is truthy; no symmetric cache-clear path exists for signed-out transitions.
`useToolSettings.ts` uses `getCachedUserId()` for fetch initialization, so stale cache directly affects scope/user resolution.

issues.summary: Auth/session lifecycle logic is duplicated across multiple hooks/components instead of a single orchestration boundary.
issues.suggestion: Introduce a single auth lifecycle service/provider exposing normalized auth state and event phases (initial session restored, signed-in, signed-out), and move page/header-specific reactions to pure subscribers with no duplicate session bootstrap logic.
evidence: `AuthContext` performs session bootstrap plus auth event debouncing/subscription.
`useHomeAuth` independently performs session fetch, hash-token session restoration, subscription, redirect decisions, and referral linking.
`useGlobalHeaderAuth` independently fetches session, subscribes to auth changes, and drives user-related derived fetches.
Each module owns overlapping auth initialization concerns with distinct timing and side effects, increasing drift risk.

issues.summary: `useHomeAuth` bundles unrelated concerns (OAuth parsing, PWA routing, referral side effects, and auth listening) into one effect-heavy controller.
issues.suggestion: Split into composable units: token-restoration adapter, auth state subscription hook, and navigation policy hook; keep referral linking in a dedicated post-signin effect/service with explicit success/failure contract.
evidence: Single hook manages URL hash parsing, `setSessionFromTokens`, immediate/delayed `fetchCurrentSession`, standalone detection, redirect policy, and referral RPC sequencing.
Nested async closures and localStorage flags (`oauthInProgress`, referral keys) are embedded directly in auth event callback paths.
Behavior overlaps with other auth consumers, indicating this hook acts as both policy engine and low-level transport client.

issues.summary: Monolithic generated Supabase type module creates high coupling and low navigability for domain code.
issues.suggestion: Decompose exported type surface into stable domain-specific type entrypoints (e.g., `types/json.ts`, `types/database-public.ts`, generated base kept internal), then migrate imports to narrow modules to reduce transitive type coupling.
evidence: `src/integrations/supabase/types.ts` remains 2576 LOC.
Both low-level runtime and higher-level domain hooks import from the same large type surface (`Database`, `Json`).
Mechanical complexity hotspot includes this file and adjacent modules, indicating centralization pressure.

issues.summary: Clip duration hydration runs uncancelled async updates that can apply after state/context shifts, creating coherence drift in clip state.
issues.suggestion: Apply the same versioning/cancellation strategy used in hydration effects to duration-loading updates (request token ref + stale-check before `setClips`), or move duration hydration into a reducer/state machine that rejects stale actions.
evidence: The `useEffect` for `getClipsNeedingDuration(clips)` launches `forEach(async clip => ...)` with no cancellation/version guard.
On project or clip list changes, late `loadClipDuration` resolutions still call `setClips`, potentially writing stale metadata flags/durations into newer state.
Earlier hydration effects in the same file already use request-version guards, but duration loading path does not, showing inconsistent orchestration patterns.

issues.summary: Realtime connection is instantiated as a module singleton with constructor side effects, coupling runtime behavior to import timing.
issues.suggestion: Replace eager singleton export with explicit factory/registry initialization during app bootstrap (similar to Supabase runtime bootstrap), and inject connection instance into provider/context to decouple import order from side-effect registration.
evidence: `RealtimeConnection.ts` exports `realtimeConnection = new RealtimeConnection()` at module scope.
Constructor immediately registers window app-event listener (`realtime:auth-heal`) when `window` exists.
Provider and other imports consume this singleton directly, so initialization semantics are implicit rather than controlled via explicit runtime bootstrap.

issues.summary: Resource update path leaks ownership/existence details for IDs not owned by caller
issues.suggestion: Remove the unauthenticated existence probe and always perform a single user-scoped lookup/update. Return a uniform error for both missing and unauthorized resources, and stop logging owner IDs in client-visible error paths.
evidence: `useUpdateResource` performs an existence probe without `user_id` constraint before auth-scoped verification (`select id,user_id,type where id = ...`).
When ownership check fails it throws differentiated messages (`This resource belongs to another user` vs generic not found), enabling ID probing semantics.

issues.summary: Resource listing hooks silently cap results at 20k but expose them as complete lists
issues.suggestion: Make the contract explicit: either expose cursor/paginated APIs to consumers or return a `truncated` flag/error when the cap is hit. Update `useResourceBrowserData` to handle incomplete datasets explicitly.
evidence: `useListPublicResources` stops when `allData.length >= 20000` by forcing `hasMore = false`; `useListResources` breaks on the same threshold.
Neither hook returns truncation metadata/error; downstream UI/filtering logic assumes completeness and can hide valid resources beyond the cap.

issues.summary: Hook mutates state during render to reset pagination, obscuring update flow
issues.suggestion: Move page reset into explicit state transition handlers (`toggleMyResourcesFilter`/`clearMyResourcesFilter`) or an effect keyed on `showMyResourcesOnly`.
evidence: `useResourceBrowserData` compares a ref and executes `setCurrentPage(1)` directly in render when `showMyResourcesOnly` changes (lines 66-70).

This creates render-driven state transitions rather than event/effect-driven transitions, making behavior harder to reason about and increasing risk of accidental re-render loops when adjacent state logic changes.

issues.summary: Persisted transform payload is cast to `ImageTransform` without runtime validation
issues.suggestion: Introduce a runtime decoder/guard for transform payloads (number finite checks + boolean checks + defaults) and centralize coercion before state hydration.
evidence: `useImageTransform` reads `activeVariantParams?.transform as ImageTransform` from `Record<string, unknown>` and only checks `typeof savedTransform === 'object'`.
Values are then consumed numerically in `useCanvasTransform` (`translate`, `scale`, `rotate`) without sanitization, so malformed persisted values can produce invalid transforms/NaN behavior.
